skill.name: agentdb-semantic-vector-search
skill.description: Build semantic vector search systems with AgentDB for document retrieval, RAG applications, and knowledge bases.
skill.summary: Build AgentDB semantic search workflows for vector retrieval, RAG, and knowledge bases.
skill.author: DNYoussef

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported 19 potential issues, but review found no malicious behavior or prompt injection. Most external command detections are Markdown code fences or TypeScript examples; the only real command use is a documented npm quick start. Hardcoded URLs are documentation links, and the weak cryptography and reconnaissance alerts are false positives.

low_findings.title: Documented npm Command Requires User Execution
low_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE for command documentation. README.md shows npm install and npx ts-node commands, but the skill does not execute them automatically. Risk is limited to normal dependency and script review before use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The shell commands are directly visible in a bash quick-start block. Confidence is moderate because this is legitimate documentation, not automatic execution by the skill.

low_findings.title: External Command Alerts Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE for Ruby or shell backtick execution. The flagged PROCESS.md and SKILL.md lines are Markdown code fences around TypeScript examples for AgentDB setup, embedding, indexing, search, and reranking.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is plain Markdown fenced examples, not executable files or instructions that run during skill loading. No user-controlled command interpolation is present.

low_findings.title: Documentation URLs Are Benign Network References
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE for risky network behavior. PROCESS.md and SKILL.md link to AgentDB documentation, and no code sends data or credentials to those URLs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines contain static documentation links only. There is no request code, webhook, credential handling, or data exfiltration path.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance and Weak Crypto Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE for system reconnaissance and weak cryptography. The flagged lines describe semantic search, hybrid search, or embeddings; they do not inspect host state, enumerate files, or use cryptographic algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found domain text about search features at each cited location. No filesystem probing, host inspection, MD5, SHA-1, or similar weak crypto use appears there.

content.value_statement: Teams need reliable semantic retrieval for documents and knowledge bases. This skill guides AgentDB vector setup, embeddings, indexing, APIs, and relevance tuning.
actual_capabilities: Outlines a five-phase workflow for AgentDB vector search.
Shows TypeScript examples for storing document embeddings.
Describes HNSW index creation and tuning parameters.
Provides an API query flow for semantic search endpoints.
Covers hybrid search and reranking refinement steps.
Defines success metrics for relevance and latency.
limitations: Does not include a complete runnable application.
Uses example packages and APIs that must be verified locally.
Does not manage secrets, hosting, or production access controls.
Performance targets depend on corpus size, embeddings, and infrastructure.

use_cases.title: Prototype document search
use_cases.description: Create an AgentDB workflow for embedding documents and returning relevant results.

use_cases.title: Add search APIs
use_cases.description: Plan REST endpoints that accept queries, embed text, filter metadata, and return ranked matches.

use_cases.title: Prepare a RAG backend
use_cases.description: Use vector retrieval and reranking steps to support context selection for an assistant.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a basic search system
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to outline a simple AgentDB semantic search workflow for my document collection.

prompt_templates.title: Design document ingestion
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design ingestion steps for chunking, embedding, metadata storage, and vector insertion.

prompt_templates.title: Build a query interface
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to plan a search API with query embeddings, top-k retrieval, metadata filters, and ranked responses.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize relevance and latency
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to improve a semantic search system with HNSW tuning, hybrid search, reranking, and relevance metrics.

output_examples.input: Plan semantic search for internal support articles.
output_examples.output: A phased plan covering AgentDB setup, embedding generation, vector storage, query endpoints, and relevance validation.

output_examples.input: Improve search quality for product documentation.
output_examples.output: Recommendations for metadata filters, hybrid search, reranking, and top-result relevance measurement.

output_examples.input: Prepare retrieval for a RAG assistant.
output_examples.output: A retrieval design with document chunks, embeddings, similarity search, and context selection before generation.

best_practices: Validate package names and examples before running installation commands.
Measure relevance with representative queries before tuning performance.
Store enough metadata to support filtering and result explanations.
anti_patterns: Do not assume example latency targets apply to every corpus.
Do not store sensitive document text without access controls.
Do not rely only on vector similarity when keyword precision is required.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps plan AgentDB semantic search for documents, RAG systems, and knowledge bases.

faq.question: Does it provide a complete application?
faq.answer: No. It provides workflow guidance and examples that developers adapt to their project.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it execute commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It documents setup commands, but users decide whether to run them.

faq.question: Can it support hybrid search?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill describes combining vector retrieval with keyword search and reranking.

faq.question: What should I verify before production use?
faq.answer: Verify dependencies, access controls, embedding costs, relevance metrics, and latency on real data.
